steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: x one-liter bottles contribute x × 1 = x liters.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: y three-liter bottles contribute y × 3 = 3y liters.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $x+3y=240$: x liters from one-liter bottles plus 3y liters from three-liter bottles equals 240. ✓
  • Choice B — $x+y=240$: Counts bottles, not liters; ignores that three-liter bottles contribute 3 liters each. ✗
  • Choice C — $3x+3y=240$: Incorrectly treats one-liter bottles as three-liter bottles. ✗
  • Choice D — $3x+y=240$: Swaps the coefficients; one-liter bottles get a factor of 3 and three-liter bottles get a factor of 1. ✗
That is the correct answer.
